title: Citrusy Crunchy Argentinian Chicken Skillet
description: A hearty and flavorful Argentinian-inspired chicken skillet dish, perfect for a sick day, featuring a citrusy and crunchy texture, with a Mediterranean twist. This dish is made with soda water, sautéed to perfection, and packed with nutrients.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned, about 5-6 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside.
Add the garlic, red bell pepper, and lemon juice to the skillet and cook until the pepper is tender, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the soda water to the skillet and stir to deglaze, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
Add the chicken back to the skillet and stir to coat with the citrus sauce.
In a small bowl, mix together the panko breadcrumbs and parsley.
Sprinkle the breadcrumb mixture over the top of the chicken and cook until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
